step_by_step_thinking:
Alginate gels form rapidly when mixed with calcium chloride or other divalent cations. The setting time can vary based on concentration but typically ranges from about 3 to 4 minutes for standard concentrations used in dental impressions. At room temperature (around 20°C), these times hold true without significant alteration unless specified otherwise by manufacturer instructions.

My answer is A: 3-4 mins. Your analysis accurately reflects the typical behavior of alginate gelling within this timeframe under normal conditions.
